name: Pizza Uno's Deep Pan Chicago Style Pizza
recipeIngredient: 1 lb Sausage, removed from the casing and crumbled
28 oz Can whole tomatoes, drained and coarsely crushed
3 tsp Dried oregano -or- 5 fresh basil leaves, shredded
recipeInstructions:
Crust:
1. Pour warm water into a large mixing bowl and dissolve yeast with a fork.
2. Add 1 cup flour, all cornmeal, salt, and oil. Mix well with a spoon.
3. Continue stirring in the remaining flour (1/2 cup at a time) until the dough comes away from the sides of the bowl.
4. Flour hands and work surface; knead the dough ball until no longer sticky.
5. Let the dough rise in an oiled bowl, sealed with plastic wrap, for 45 to 60 minutes in a warm place until doubled in bulk.
6. Punch down and briefly knead the dough. Press it into an oiled 15-inch deep dish pizza pan until it comes 2 inches up the sides and is even on the bottom of the pan. Let rise 15-20 minutes before filling.
1. Preheat oven to 500°F.
2. While the dough is rising, cook crumbled sausage until no longer pink, drain excess fat, drain and chop tomatoes.
3. Lay cheese over dough shell. Distribute sausage and garlic over cheese. Top with tomatoes. Sprinkle seasonings and Parmesan cheese.
Baking:
1. Bake for 15 minutes at 500°F.
2. Lower temperature to 400°F and bake for 25 to 35 minutes longer, lifting crust occasionally with a spatula to check color.
3. Crust is golden brown when done. Serve immediately.
